FrenchOlympianJules Bouyer has made a sizeable splash in Paris, but not because he’s a 3m springboard diver.

The hunky athlete caught the eye of viewers globally when footage emerged of his swim trunks appearing to barely contain his manhood following a dive.

Upon closer inspection, his hardworking Speedos were made by Australian brand Budgy Smuggler, and not the more aptly named French sports clothing brand Le Coq Sportif.

Naturally, the internet has weighed in on the 22-year-old’s situation.

“I just really hope his speedo is getting paid time and a half,” one X user said.

“He could do anything to me and I’d still worship him like a god,” another commented.

Others suggested their interest in the Games relied solely upon the attractive diver.

“IDGAF about the Olympics can someone just tell me when French diver Jules Bouyer is on TV,” one person joked.

Jules Bouyer and diving parter Alexis Jandard hope to achieve gold in the final of the men’s synchronised 3m springboard final on August 2.

‘So shameless’

title: French diver Jules Bouyer makes a splash as his revealing Speedo turns heads at Paris Olympics | PerthNow
description: A French diver has sent the internet into a frenzy after footage emerged of his hardworking Speedos battling to contain his manhood.
